# AI Description

# Technical Description: Figure A-8.4.4.17.2 - Fan Performance Curves

## 1. Diagram Type
This is a **performance curve graph** showing the relationship between fan power consumption and load conditions for various fan system configurations.

## 2. Building Components/Systems Shown
The diagram illustrates **fan system performance characteristics** for different fan configurations and control strategies, specifically:
- Air foil or backward inclined riding fan curves
- Air foil or backward inclined fans with inlet vanes
- Forward curved fans with inlet vanes
- Variable speed drive systems

## 3. Visible Labels, Measurements, and Annotations

### Axes:
- **X-axis**: F = F_partload / F_rated (Fan load ratio, ranging from 0 to 1.0)
- **Y-axis**: P = P_partload / P_rated (Power ratio, ranging from 0 to 1.2)

### Curve Labels:
- **(a)** - Dashed line (- - -): air foil or backward inclined riding fan curve
  - Starting point: approximately 0.7 at F=0
  - Ending point: 1.0 at F=1.0
  
- **(b)** - Solid line (___): air foil or backward inclined with inlet vanes
  - Starting point: approximately 0.5 at F=0
  - Ending point: 1.0 at F=1.0
  
- **(c)** - Dash-dot line (- · - · -): forward curved with inlet vanes
  - Starting point: approximately 0.2 at F=0
  - Ending point: 1.0 at F=1.0
  
- **(d)** - Long dash line (- - -): variable speed drive
  - Starting point: approximately 0.05 at F=0
  - Ending point: 1.0 at F=1.0

### Document Reference:
- **Code Reference**: A-8.4.4.20.(4)(a)
- **Division**: Division B
- **Document Code**: EG02002B

## 4. Relationships and Connections Between Elements

The graph demonstrates the **part-load power consumption characteristics** of different fan control strategies:

- **Curve (a)** shows the least efficient part-load performance, maintaining approximately 70% power consumption even at zero load
- **Curve (b)** shows improved efficiency with inlet vane control, reducing to approximately 50% power at zero load
- **Curve (c)** demonstrates better part-load efficiency with forward curved fans and inlet vanes, dropping to approximately 20% power at zero load
- **Curve (d)** exhibits the most efficient part-load performance using variable speed drives, approaching near-zero power consumption at zero load

All curves converge at the point (1.0, 1.0), representing **rated full-load conditions**.

The curves illustrate that **variable speed drives (VSD)** provide the most energy-efficient control strategy for part-load operation, with power consumption closely following the cube law relationship with flow reduction.

## 5. Compliance Requirements and Technical Specifications

This figure appears in **Appendix A** of the NECB, providing guidance for:

- **Fan system selection** based on part-load efficiency characteristics
- **Energy modeling** of HVAC fan systems at various load conditions
- **Comparison of control strategies** for fan power consumption
- **Justification for variable speed drive selection** in energy efficiency calculations

The diagram supports compliance calculations under **Section 8.4.4** (likely related to HVAC system efficiency requirements) by providing standardized performance curves for different fan control methods. Engineers can use these curves to:
- Calculate annual energy consumption for fan systems
- Compare energy performance of different fan control strategies
- Demonstrate compliance with fan power limitations
- Support trade-off calculations in performance path compliance

This reference data is critical for **energy modeling software** and **mechanical design** to accurately represent part-load fan performance in building energy simulations.
